item.title: [7] Tuning spin currents in collinear antiferromagnets and altermagnets
item.description:
Last authors: Sajjan Sheoran, Pratibha Dev

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Directly addresses altermagnetism, a specifically listed area of interest for the user.

Tags: #altermagnetism #spintronics

Abstract: Author(s): Sajjan Sheoran and Pratibha Dev Spin-current generation through nonrelativistic spin splittings, found in uncompensated magnets and $d$-wave altermagnets (AMs), is desirable for low-power spintronics. Such spin currents, however, are symmetry forbidden in conventional collinear antiferromagnets and higher-order AMs. Using spin-poi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174426] Published Tue May 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Sajjan Sheoran, Pratibha Dev

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Directly addresses altermagnetism, a specifically listed area of interest for the user.

Tags: #altermagnetism #spintronics

Abstract: Author(s): Sajjan Sheoran and Pratibha Dev Spin-current generation through nonrelativistic spin splittings, found in uncompensated magnets and $d$-wave altermagnets (AMs), is desirable for low-power spintronics. Such spin currents, however, are symmetry forbidden in conventional collinear antiferromagnets and higher-order AMs. Using spin-poi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174426] Published Tue May 19, 2026

item.dc:creator: Sajjan Sheoran, Pratibha Dev
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Accessing dirty-regime anomalous Hall effect in pure ferromagnetic metals
item.description:
Last authors: Wenyu Xing, Weibo Gao

Authors: Tengfei Ma, Lingsong Huang, Ning Jiang, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of the anomalous Hall effect, which is relevant to the user's topology interests.

Tags: #anomalousHall #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Tengfei Ma, Lingsong Huang, Ning Jiang, Yaoxiang Jiang, Guixin He, Shifeng Zhao, Cong Wang, Wenyu Xing, and Weibo Gao The anomalous Hall effect (AHE) in the dirty regime of pure ferromagnetic metals is challenging to access without doping. Here, the authors use cluster beam deposition to tune structural disorder in ferromagnetic nanogranular CoFe films, reaching a low- conductivity metallic state and enabling systematic study of the AHE in the dirty regime. The AHE is dominated by the intrinsic mechanism and follows the predicted scaling σ AHE ≈ σ x x 1 . 6 . This work fills a key experimental gap and establishes a disorder-based platform for engineering the AHE. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174428] Published Tue May 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Wenyu Xing, Weibo Gao

Authors: Tengfei Ma, Lingsong Huang, Ning Jiang, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of the anomalous Hall effect, which is relevant to the user's topology interests.

Tags: #anomalousHall #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Tengfei Ma, Lingsong Huang, Ning Jiang, Yaoxiang Jiang, Guixin He, Shifeng Zhao, Cong Wang, Wenyu Xing, and Weibo Gao The anomalous Hall effect (AHE) in the dirty regime of pure ferromagnetic metals is challenging to access without doping. Here, the authors use cluster beam deposition to tune structural disorder in ferromagnetic nanogranular CoFe films, reaching a low- conductivity metallic state and enabling systematic study of the AHE in the dirty regime. The AHE is dominated by the intrinsic mechanism and follows the predicted scaling σ AHE ≈ σ x x 1 . 6 . This work fills a key experimental gap and establishes a disorder-based platform for engineering the AHE. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174428] Published Tue May 19, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Accessing dirty-regime anomalous Hall effect in pure ferromagnetic metals
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Wenyu Xing, Weibo Gao; Authors: Tengfei Ma, Lingsong Huang, Ning Jiang, et al.

item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Ultrafast charge doping via photothermionic injection in van der Waals devices
item.description:
Last authors: Eric A. Arsenault, Xiaoyang Zhu

Authors: Yiliu Li, Esteban Rojas-Gatjens, Yinjie Guo, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study on van der Waals and moiré devices, relevant to the user's 2D materials interest.

Tags: #vanderWaals #moiré #2Dmaterials

Abstract: Author(s): Yiliu Li, Esteban Rojas-Gatjens, Yinjie Guo, Birui Yang, Dihao Sun, Luke Holtzman, Juseung Oh, Katayun Barmak, Cory R. Dean, James C. Hone, Nathaniel Gabor, Eric A. Arsenault, and Xiaoyang Zhu The authors present here a quantitative study of an ultrafast photodoping mechanism from photothermionic emission of the graphite gates in a prototypical dual-gated moiré van der Waals device. This mechanism enables ultrafast and tunable control of carrier density in active layers in vdW structures. Furthermore, this work demonstrates the potential of utilizing the full vdW device stack to probe and engineer the nonequilibrium dynamics of quantum phases in two-dimensional materials. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195135] Published Tue May 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Eric A. Arsenault, Xiaoyang Zhu

Authors: Yiliu Li, Esteban Rojas-Gatjens, Yinjie Guo, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study on van der Waals and moiré devices, relevant to the user's 2D materials interest.

Tags: #vanderWaals #moiré #2Dmaterials

Abstract: Author(s): Yiliu Li, Esteban Rojas-Gatjens, Yinjie Guo, Birui Yang, Dihao Sun, Luke Holtzman, Juseung Oh, Katayun Barmak, Cory R. Dean, James C. Hone, Nathaniel Gabor, Eric A. Arsenault, and Xiaoyang Zhu The authors present here a quantitative study of an ultrafast photodoping mechanism from photothermionic emission of the graphite gates in a prototypical dual-gated moiré van der Waals device. This mechanism enables ultrafast and tunable control of carrier density in active layers in vdW structures. Furthermore, this work demonstrates the potential of utilizing the full vdW device stack to probe and engineer the nonequilibrium dynamics of quantum phases in two-dimensional materials. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195135] Published Tue May 19, 2026

item.dc:creator: Last authors: Eric A. Arsenault, Xiaoyang Zhu; Authors: Yiliu Li, Esteban Rojas-Gatjens, Yinjie Guo, et al.

item.title: [8] Switchable axionic magnetoelectric effect via spin-flop transition in topological antiferromagnets
item.description:
Last authors: Huaiqiang Wang, Haijun Zhang

Authors: Yiliang Fan, Rongxiang Zhu, Tongshuai Zhu, Jianzhou Zhao, Huaiqiang Wang, Haijun Zhang

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Explores MnBi2Te4 and axion physics in topological magnets, highly relevant material class.

Tags: #topologicalmagnet #axioninsulator #MnBi2Te4

Abstract: Author(s): Yiliang Fan, Rongxiang Zhu, Tongshuai Zhu, Jianzhou Zhao, Huaiqiang Wang, and Haijun Zhang The ${\mathrm{MnBi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$ material family has emerged as a key platform for exploring magnetic topological phases, most notably exemplified by the experimental realization of the axion insulator state. The interplay between spin dynamics and axion states in topological magnets is an… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195137] Published Tue May 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Huaiqiang Wang, Haijun Zhang

Authors: Yiliang Fan, Rongxiang Zhu, Tongshuai Zhu, Jianzhou Zhao, Huaiqiang Wang, Haijun Zhang

The interplay between spin dynamics and axion states in topological magnets is an… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195137] Published Tue May 19, 2026

item.title: [7] Ferroelectric switching of the anomalous Hall effect in two-dimensional multiferroics: Symmetry criteria and material validation
item.description:
Last authors: Churen Gui, Wei-Bing Zhang

Authors: Shu-Zong Li, Zhixiong Yang, Zhenqing Li, Churen Gui, Wei-Bing Zhang

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Symmetry-based study of anomalous Hall effect in 2D materials, aligning with the user's topological interests.

Tags: #anomalousHall #2Dmaterials #topology

Abstract: Author(s): Shu-Zong Li, Zhixiong Yang, Zhenqing Li, Churen Gui, and Wei-Bing Zhang The reversal of the anomalous Hall effect (AHE) via low-power ferroelectric (FE) switching has recently attracted significant interest. However, a general symmetry-based design principle for such control remains absent. Here, we establish effective symmetry criteria for FE-switchable AHE in two-dime… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195431] Published Tue May 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:

However, a general symmetry-based design principle for such control remains absent. Here, we establish effective symmetry criteria for FE-switchable AHE in two-dime… [Phys. Rev. item.title: [7] Spin responses of a disordered helical superconducting edge under a Zeeman field
item.description:
Last authors: Zeinab Bakhshipour, Mir Vahid Hosseini

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretic study of topological insulator edges and superconductivity, relevant to the user's TI/TSC interests.

Tags: #topologicalinsulator #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Zeinab Bakhshipour and Mir Vahid Hosseini We investigate analytically and numerically the effects of disorder on the helical edge of a two-dimensional topological insulator in the presence of a Zeeman field and superconductivity. Employing bosonization and a renormalization-group analysis, we study how impurity potentials modify charge- and… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205135] Published Tue May 19, 2026

item.title: [8] Critical structural parameter determining magnetic phases in the Fe2Mo3O8 altermagnet system
item.description:
Last authors: Sang-Wook Cheong, AM Milinda Abeykoon

Authors: Trevor A. Tyson, Sizhan Liu, Sandun Amarasinghe, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental investigation of a specific altermagnet system, directly relevant to the user's research interests.

Tags: #altermagnetism #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Trevor A. Tyson, Sizhan Liu, Sandun Amarasinghe, Kefeng Wang, Stella Chariton, Vitali Prakapenka, Tieyan Chang, Yu-Sheng Chen, Zhenxian Liu, Christopher J. Pollock, Sang-Wook Cheong, and AM Milinda Abeykoon A systematic structural investigation of $\mathrm{F}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}\mathrm{M}{\mathrm{o}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{8}$ as a function of pressure, temperature, and magnetic field reveals that the $P{6}_{3}mc$ space group remains robust over a wide range of conditions. No changes in the long-range crystal st… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174112] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Sang-Wook Cheong, AM Milinda Abeykoon

Pollock, Sang-Wook Cheong, and AM Milinda Abeykoon A systematic structural investigation of $\mathrm{F}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}\mathrm{M}{\mathrm{o}}_{3}{\mathrm{O}}_{8}$ as a function of pressure, temperature, and magnetic field reveals that the $P{6}_{3}mc$ space group remains robust over a wide range of conditions. No changes in the long-range crystal st… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174112] Published Mon May 18, 2026

Tags: #nickelates #thinfilms

Abstract: Author(s): Tomoki Deguchi, Takeshi Hara, Yuta Ishii, Hao-Bo Li, Azusa N. Hattori, Hidekazu Tanaka, Hajime Sagayama, and Yusuke Wakabayashi Hydrogen doping offers a versatile method to dynamically control the doping level of metal oxides over a wide range, yet it introduces strong lattice modulation whose structural details have been poorly investigated. Here, we elucidate the hydrogen-induced structural modulation in ${\mathrm{NdNiO}}_… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174113] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [9] X-ray linear dichroism in the kagome antiferromagnet Mn3Sn
item.description:
Last authors: Satoru Nakatsuji, Shinji Miwa

Authors: Shoya Sakamoto, Tomoya Higo, Kenta Amemiya, Satoru Nakatsuji, Shinji Miwa

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Direct spectroscopic probe of electronic/magnetic order in a high-interest kagome system.

Tags: #kagome #Mn3Sn #dichroism

Abstract: Author(s): Shoya Sakamoto, Tomoya Higo, Kenta Amemiya, Satoru Nakatsuji, and Shinji Miwa We report the observation of x-ray linear dichroism (XLD) at the Mn ${L}_{2,3}$ edges in an epitaxial W/${\mathrm{Mn}}_{3}\mathrm{Sn}$ thin film with kagome planes oriented out of plane. A finite XLD signal is observed between linear polarizations parallel and perpendicular to the kagome planes, ref… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174418] Published Mon May 18, 2026

A finite XLD signal is observed between linear polarizations parallel and perpendicular to the kagome planes, ref… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174418] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [7] Observation of Bethe strings in the quantum spin chain antiferromagnet BaCo2V2O8
item.description:
Last authors: Jianda Wu, Bella Lake

Authors: Konrad Puzniak, A. T. M. Nazmul Islam, Xiaotong Chen, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Neutron scattering study of 1D quantum magnetic excitations.

Tags: #1D #neutron-scattering #spin-chain

Abstract: Author(s): Konrad Puzniak, A. T. M. Nazmul Islam, Xiaotong Chen, Jiahao Yang, Paul Steffens, Martin Boehm, Jianda Wu, and Bella Lake Bethe strings, complex bound states of magnetic excitations, are a fundamental yet long-sought exotic states of 1D quantum Heisenberg magnets. Various string states are observed via inelastic neutron scattering measurements in the spin-chain compound BaCo 2 V 2 O 8 in an applied longitudinal magnetic field. The experimental data exhibit quantitatively excellent agreement with theoretical predictions across a wide field range, providing unambiguous validation of Bethe string excitations. These results reveal the underlying spectrum, including at high energies, of spin chains in the quantum critical region. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174419] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Jianda Wu, Bella Lake

Authors: Konrad Puzniak, A. T. M. Nazmul Islam, Xiaotong Chen, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Neutron scattering study of 1D quantum magnetic excitations.

Tags: #1D #neutron-scattering #spin-chain

Abstract: Author(s): Konrad Puzniak, A. T. M. Nazmul Islam, Xiaotong Chen, Jiahao Yang, Paul Steffens, Martin Boehm, Jianda Wu, and Bella Lake Bethe strings, complex bound states of magnetic excitations, are a fundamental yet long-sought exotic states of 1D quantum Heisenberg magnets. Various string states are observed via inelastic neutron scattering measurements in the spin-chain compound BaCo 2 V 2 O 8 in an applied longitudinal magnetic field. The experimental data exhibit quantitatively excellent agreement with theoretical predictions across a wide field range, providing unambiguous validation of Bethe string excitations. These results reveal the underlying spectrum, including at high energies, of spin chains in the quantum critical region. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174419] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [8] Anisotropic metamagnetism and magnetotransport of heavy rare-earth orthorhombic single-crystal TbAlGe
item.description:
Last authors: S. R. Saha, J. Paglione

Authors: Ram Kumar, K. E. Avers, V. Saini, et al.

Tags: #topological-semimetal #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Ram Kumar, K. E. Avers, V. Saini, D. S. Sokratov, Y. Anand, P. Saraf, J. A. Horn, N. Brenowitz, S. Otazo, P. Sobel, D. Graf, S. R. Saha, and J. Paglione We report a comprehensive investigation of the anisotropic magnetism and magnetic field-induced transitions in single crystals of the orthorhombic system TbAlGe, a member of the topological $R\mathrm{AlGe}$ (where $R$ denotes rare earth) family with the highest ordering temeprature in the $R\mathrm{… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174423] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [9] Controlling correlations of a polaritonic Luttinger liquid by engineered cross-Kerr nonlinearity
item.description:
Last authors: Anushree Dey, Bimalendu Deb

Authors: Nabaneet Sharma, Anushree Dey, Bimalendu Deb

Score 9/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: title strong match: Luttinger liquid

Tags: #Luttinger #superconduct

Abstract: Author(s): Nabaneet Sharma, Anushree Dey, and Bimalendu Deb We study correlation properties of polaritons at zero temperature in a multiconnected Jaynes-Cummings lattice on a superconducting circuit quantum electrodynamics platform with engineered cross-Kerr nonlinearity that mimics attractive nearest-neighbor interaction. A multiconnected Jaynes-Cummings la… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174520] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [7] Variational Monte Carlo study on the bilayer t−J∥−J⊥ model for La3Ni2O7
item.description:
Last authors: Yu-Bo Liu, Fan Yang

Authors: Zeyu Chen, Yu-Bo Liu, Fan Yang

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Pairing mechanism theory for high-interest nickelate superconductors.

Tags: #nickelate #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Zeyu Chen, Yu-Bo Liu, and Fan Yang The discovery of high-temperature superconductivity (HTSC) in ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$ has aroused significant interest in exploring the pairing mechanism. Previous studies have proposed an effective ${d}_{{x}^{2}−{y}^{2}}$-orbital bilayer $t−{J}_{∥}−{J}_{⊥}$ model, in wh… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174521] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Yu-Bo Liu, Fan Yang

Authors: Zeyu Chen, Yu-Bo Liu, Fan Yang

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Pairing mechanism theory for high-interest nickelate superconductors.

Tags: #nickelate #superconductivity

Abstract: Author(s): Zeyu Chen, Yu-Bo Liu, and Fan Yang The discovery of high-temperature superconductivity (HTSC) in ${\mathrm{La}}_{3}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{2}{\mathrm{O}}_{7}$ has aroused significant interest in exploring the pairing mechanism. Previous studies have proposed an effective ${d}_{{x}^{2}−{y}^{2}}$-orbital bilayer $t−{J}_{∥}−{J}_{⊥}$ model, in wh… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 174521] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Variational Monte Carlo study on the bilayer t−J∥−J⊥ model for La3Ni2O7
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Yu-Bo Liu, Fan Yang; Authors: Zeyu Chen, Yu-Bo Liu, Fan Yang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [8] Nonvolatile control of nonlinear Hall and circular photogalvanic effects via Berry curvature dipole in multiferroic monolayer CrNBr2
item.description:
Last authors: Guibo Zheng, Fangping Ouyang

Authors: Wenzhe Zhou, Yinheng Li, Dehe Zhang, Guibo Zheng, Fangping Ouyang

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Monolayer material study focusing on Berry curvature effects and transport.

Tags: #Berry-curvature #2D-materials #Hall-effect

Abstract: Author(s): Wenzhe Zhou, Yinheng Li, Dehe Zhang, Guibo Zheng, and Fangping Ouyang The Berry curvature dipole induced by symmetry breaking plays a pivotal role in electronic transport properties and nonlinear responses, such as the nonlinear Hall effect and circular photogalvanic effect. The study of the Berry curvature dipole is often explored in time-reversal symmetric systems, … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195126] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [7] Dispersive quasiparticles in the band gap of the intermetallic semiconductor FeGa3 due to an ordered secondary phase
item.description:
Last authors: Shuai Dong, Lin Miao

Authors: Quan Ren, Ning Ding, Ruoqi Wang, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of quasiparticle excitations and electronic structure in a quantum-critical semiconductor.

Tags: #electronic-structure #quantum-critical

Abstract: Author(s): Quan Ren, Ning Ding, Ruoqi Wang, Haonan Chen, Wenting Lin, Huayao Li, Kaidi Xu, Lulu Han, Shanshan Wang, Si Zhang, Guohua Wang, Taishi Chen, Cheng Zhang, Xiaoqian Zhang, Shuai Dong, and Lin Miao The narrow-gap semiconductor ${\mathrm{FeGa}}_{3}$ has attracted considerable attention due to its colossal thermoelectric Seebeck effect, intriguing magnetic anomaly, and quantum criticality. Although the quasiparticle excitations in the band gap have been probed and proposed to be directly associa… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195128] Published Mon May 18, 2026

Last authors: Shuai Dong, Lin Miao

Authors: Quan Ren, Ning Ding, Ruoqi Wang, et al.

Although the quasiparticle excitations in the band gap have been probed and proposed to be directly associa… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195128] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [7] Abelian and non-Abelian fractionalized states in twisted MoTe2: A generalized Landau-level theory
item.description:
Last authors: Yunze Ouyang, Fengcheng Wu

Authors: Bohao Li, Yunze Ouyang, Fengcheng Wu

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical characterization of bands in high-interest twisted moiré systems.

Tags: #moire #twisted-bilayer #Chern

Abstract: Author(s): Bohao Li, Yunze Ouyang, and Fengcheng Wu Here, the authors develop a framework that variationally decomposes Bloch bands into generalized Landau levels (LLs), enabling a quantitative characterization of their effective LL nature. Applied to twisted bilayer MoTe 2 , the first band is dominated by the generalized zeroth LL, supporting Abelian fractional Chern insulators. The second band, at specific twist angles, is dominated by the generalized first LL and exhibits numerical evidence consistent with a non-Abelian Moore–Read state. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195129] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Yunze Ouyang, Fengcheng Wu

Authors: Bohao Li, Yunze Ouyang, Fengcheng Wu

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical characterization of bands in high-interest twisted moiré systems.

Tags: #moire #twisted-bilayer #Chern

Abstract: Author(s): Bohao Li, Yunze Ouyang, and Fengcheng Wu Here, the authors develop a framework that variationally decomposes Bloch bands into generalized Landau levels (LLs), enabling a quantitative characterization of their effective LL nature. Applied to twisted bilayer MoTe 2 , the first band is dominated by the generalized zeroth LL, supporting Abelian fractional Chern insulators. The second band, at specific twist angles, is dominated by the generalized first LL and exhibits numerical evidence consistent with a non-Abelian Moore–Read state. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195129] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Abelian and non-Abelian fractionalized states in twisted MoTe2: A generalized Landau-level theory
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Yunze Ouyang, Fengcheng Wu; Authors: Bohao Li, Yunze Ouyang, Fengcheng Wu

Tags: #CDW #NbSe3 #quasi-1D

Abstract: Author(s): Yongsheng Zhao, Konstantin Glazyrin, Pablo J. Bereciartua, Christian Plueckthun, Satishkumar Kulkarni, Nana Li, Limin Yan, Kai Zhang, Bihan Wang, Yiming Wang, Jianbo Zhang, Nico Giordano, Helmuth Berger, Xin Wang, Sonia Francoual, Wenge Yang, and Moritz Hoesch Controlling collective electronic states through clean and reversible external stimuli is crucial for realizing functional quantum materials. Here, we report a study on temperature ($T$) and pressure ($P$)-tuned charge-density-wave (CDW) orders in the quasi-one-dimensional system of $\mathrm{NbS}{\m… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195130] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.title: [7] Pressure-driven metal-insulator transition in BaMn2P2: A correlated Mn-pnictide analog of Fe-based superconductors
item.description:
Last authors: Timur Kim, Henrik M. Rønnow

Authors: Youngro Lee, Daniil Evtushynsky, Richard Gaal, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Correlated pnictide system related to user's interest in unconventional superconductivity and iron-based analogs.

Tags: #pnictide #superconductivity #correlated

Abstract: Author(s): Youngro Lee, Daniil Evtushynsky, Richard Gaal, Igor Morozov, Anita Maksutova, Tomasz Poreba, Timur Kim, and Henrik M. Rønnow We report pressure-induced metallization in ${\mathrm{BaMn}}_{2}{\mathrm{P}}_{2}$ based on resistivity measurements in a diamond anvil cell. At ambient pressure, the temperature-dependent resistivity is well described by a two-gap Arrhenius model, yielding intrinsic and extrinsic activation energies… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195131] Published Mon May 18, 2026

Abstract: Author(s): Chao Mu, Jiabin Song, Le Wang, Youguo Shi, Yi-feng Yang, Zheng Li, and Jianlin Luo We report $^{59}\mathrm{Co}$ and $^{71}\mathrm{Ga}$ nuclear magnetic resonance investigations of the quasi-one-dimensional Kondo lattice compound ${\mathrm{CeCo}}_{2}{\mathrm{Ga}}_{8}$. Below the coherence temperature, ${T}^{*}≈\phantom{\rule{4pt}{0ex}}29\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\mathrm{K}$, the … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195134] Published Mon May 18, 2026

item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: [7] Nonlinear Hall effect induced by two-frequency drives
item.description:
Last authors: Rui Chen, Bin Zhou

Authors: Jiong-Yi Zhu, Rui Chen, Bin Zhou

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Explores Berry curvature dipole and Dirac systems, relevant to topological transport interests.

Tags: #Berrycurvature #Dirac #Halleffect

Abstract: Author(s): Jiong-Yi Zhu, Rui Chen, and Bin Zhou Engineering light-matter interactions with two-frequency drives unlocks a new topological response. Here, the authors demonstrate that a commensurate drive with a frequency ratio of two intrinsically breaks inversion symmetry in 2D Dirac systems, inducing a tunable nonlinear Hall effect. Crucially, the resulting Berry curvature dipole is an even function of Fermi energy, different from the odd dependent relationship found in conventional tilted systems. This approach establishes a highly controllable route for manipulating nonlinear transport in quantum materials. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195422] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Rui Chen, Bin Zhou

Authors: Jiong-Yi Zhu, Rui Chen, Bin Zhou

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Explores Berry curvature dipole and Dirac systems, relevant to topological transport interests.

Tags: #Berrycurvature #Dirac #Halleffect

Abstract: Author(s): Jiong-Yi Zhu, Rui Chen, and Bin Zhou Engineering light-matter interactions with two-frequency drives unlocks a new topological response. Here, the authors demonstrate that a commensurate drive with a frequency ratio of two intrinsically breaks inversion symmetry in 2D Dirac systems, inducing a tunable nonlinear Hall effect. Crucially, the resulting Berry curvature dipole is an even function of Fermi energy, different from the odd dependent relationship found in conventional tilted systems. This approach establishes a highly controllable route for manipulating nonlinear transport in quantum materials. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195422] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Nonlinear Hall effect induced by two-frequency drives
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Rui Chen, Bin Zhou; Authors: Jiong-Yi Zhu, Rui Chen, Bin Zhou
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: [8] Growth and crystallographic structure of TiTe2 on Au(111): From submonolayer structures to single- and multilayer films
item.description:
Last authors: Lutz Hammer, M. Alexander Schneider

Authors: Andreas Raabgrund, Tilman Kißlinger, Alexander Wegerich, Lutz Hammer, M. Alexander Schneider

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: STM and LEED study of a CDW-hosting TMD monolayer, highly relevant to electronic structure interests.

Tags: #STM #TMD #monolayer

Abstract: Author(s): Andreas Raabgrund, Tilman Kißlinger, Alexander Wegerich, Lutz Hammer, and M. Alexander Schneider Single-layer 1 T -TiTe 2 grown on a gold surface emerge from individual molecular building blocks. By combining STM, advanced LEED-IV, and DFT, the authors resolve here the atomic structure of epitaxial single-layer and multilayer TiTe 2 films on Au(111), which evolve from isolated TiTe 2 units with Ti atoms embedded in the topmost gold layer. The study uncovers Ti-induced restructuring of the Au surface, the formation of a moiré monolayer, and an unexpected lattice expansion persisting even in thicker TiTe 2 films. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195426] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Lutz Hammer, M. Alexander Schneider

Authors: Andreas Raabgrund, Tilman Kißlinger, Alexander Wegerich, Lutz Hammer, M. Alexander Schneider

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: STM and LEED study of a CDW-hosting TMD monolayer, highly relevant to electronic structure interests.

Tags: #STM #TMD #monolayer

Abstract: Author(s): Andreas Raabgrund, Tilman Kißlinger, Alexander Wegerich, Lutz Hammer, and M. Alexander Schneider Single-layer 1 T -TiTe 2 grown on a gold surface emerge from individual molecular building blocks. By combining STM, advanced LEED-IV, and DFT, the authors resolve here the atomic structure of epitaxial single-layer and multilayer TiTe 2 films on Au(111), which evolve from isolated TiTe 2 units with Ti atoms embedded in the topmost gold layer. The study uncovers Ti-induced restructuring of the Au surface, the formation of a moiré monolayer, and an unexpected lattice expansion persisting even in thicker TiTe 2 films. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195426] Published Mon May 18, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Growth and crystallographic structure of TiTe2 on Au(111): From submonolayer structures to single- and multilayer films
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Lutz Hammer, M. Alexander Schneider; Authors: Andreas Raabgrund, Tilman Kißlinger, Alexander Wegerich, Lutz Hammer, M. Alexander Schneider

item.description:

Why: Experimental probe of Dirac carriers in a classic topological semimetal alloy.

Tags: #Dirac #topologicalsemimetal

Abstract: Author(s): Ryota Miyazaki, Shunzhen Wang, Guanxiong Qu, Yukihiro Marui, Yuta Kobayashi, Masashi Kawaguchi, and Masamitsu Hayashi We study the current-induced magneto-optical Kerr effect (MOKE) in ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{1−x}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{x}$ semimetalic alloys. The MOKE signal is found to be the largest in pure Bi ($x=0$), exceeding that of transition metals by nearly four orders of magnitude, and decreases monotonically with increa… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L180406] Published Mon May 18, 2026

Studies in recent years, however, have revealed that antiferromagnetic (AFM) states characterized by magnetic point groups that allow ferromagnetism… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195116] Published Fri May 15, 2026

Abstract: Author(s): Longli Tian, Hongrun Zhen, Pengcheng Ma, Tianqi Wang, Yin Yang, Huancheng Yang, Fanyu Meng, Hechang Lei, Zhong-Yi Lu, and Zhonghao Liu Intercalation of $3d$ atoms into layered transition-metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) provides an effective route to tune their magnetism and electronic structure. Using photon-energy-dependent angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy, we show that Cr intercalation in $2H\text{−}{\mathrm{NbS}}_{2}$ intr… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195119] Published Fri May 15, 2026

Abstract: Author(s): Shivani Rastogi, Gaurav K. Shukla, Nidhi, Sunil Wilfred D’Souza, and Sanjay Singh Topological semimetals (TSMs), known for their topologically protected band crossings, give rise to a range of intriguing properties, including the anomalous Hall effect governed by Berry curvature in magnetic TSMs. Among these, cobalt-based magnetic TSMs, in particular, have been reported to exhibi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195121] Published Fri May 15, 2026

item.title: [9] Polarization dependency in resonant inelastic x-ray scattering
item.description:
Last authors: Fabian Wenzel, Maurits W. Haverkort

Authors: Michelangelo Tagliavini, Fabian Wenzel, Maurits W. Haverkort

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Methodological RIXS paper highly relevant to electronic structure characterization.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #RIXS

Abstract: Author(s): Michelangelo Tagliavini, Fabian Wenzel, and Maurits W. Haverkort Resonant Inelastic x-ray Scattering (RIXS) is a well-established tool for probing excitations in a wide range of materials. The measured spectra strongly depend on the scattering geometry via its influence on the polarization of the incoming and outgoing light. By employing a tensor representation o… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195122] Published Fri May 15, 2026

Abstract: Author(s): Michelangelo Tagliavini, Fabian Wenzel, and Maurits W. Haverkort Resonant Inelastic x-ray Scattering (RIXS) is a well-established tool for probing excitations in a wide range of materials. The measured spectra strongly depend on the scattering geometry via its influence on the polarization of the incoming and outgoing light. By employing a tensor representation o… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195122] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Polarization dependency in resonant inelastic x-ray scattering
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Fabian Wenzel, Maurits W. Haverkort; Authors: Michelangelo Tagliavini, Fabian Wenzel, Maurits W. Haverkort

item.title: [8] Observation of one-dimensional edge states between even and odd quintuple layers of ultrathin Bi2Te3
item.description:
Last authors: Yanzhao Liu, Jian Wang

Authors: Wei Ren, Zhen Liang, Tianheng Wei, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental observation of topological edge states in a well-known TI system.

Tags: #topologicalinsulator #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Wei Ren, Zhen Liang, Tianheng Wei, Kun Dong, Zhuoran Xu, Shichao Qi, Feitong Song, Ji Feng, Yanzhao Liu, and Jian Wang Topological insulators represent a class of quantum materials exhibiting a host of unique physical properties. As an important category of topological insulators, quantum spin Hall insulators show one-dimensional topological helical edge states. Here, we report the successful growth of the high-qual… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195420] Published Fri May 15, 2026

As an important category of topological insulators, quantum spin Hall insulators show one-dimensional topological helical edge states. Here, we report the successful growth of the high-qual… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195420] Published Fri May 15, 2026

item.title: [8] Current switching behavior mediated via hinge modes in higher-order topological phases using altermagnets
item.description:
Last authors: Amartya Pal, Arijit Saha

Authors: Minakshi Subhadarshini, Amartya Pal, Arijit Saha

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Higher-order topology and altermagnetism are very relevant to current interests.

Tags: #altermagnet #topologicalinsulator #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Minakshi Subhadarshini, Amartya Pal, and Arijit Saha We propose a theoretical framework to engineer hybrid-order and higher-order topological phases in three-dimensional topological insulators by coupling to $d$-wave altermagnets (AMs). The presence of only a ${d}_{{x}^{2}−{y}^{2}}$-type AM drives the system into a hybrid-order topological phase where… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195421] Published Fri May 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Amartya Pal, Arijit Saha

Authors: Minakshi Subhadarshini, Amartya Pal, Arijit Saha

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Higher-order topology and altermagnetism are very relevant to current interests.

Tags: #altermagnet #topologicalinsulator #topological

Abstract: Author(s): Minakshi Subhadarshini, Amartya Pal, and Arijit Saha We propose a theoretical framework to engineer hybrid-order and higher-order topological phases in three-dimensional topological insulators by coupling to $d$-wave altermagnets (AMs). The presence of only a ${d}_{{x}^{2}−{y}^{2}}$-type AM drives the system into a hybrid-order topological phase where… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195421] Published Fri May 15, 2026
